verses.text: Después de que la comunidad cae víctima de los malvados ( **94:5-7** ), oran para que el Señor haga justicia (ver **7:11,** **9:12** ).\n\n
Así como los enemigos del Señor se levantan contra él y su pueblo ( **3:7,** **93:3** ), él debe levantarse y hacer justicia contra el enemigo (ver **79:12,** **Is 65:6** ).

94:3 Lord, how long shall the wicked,.... The reign of antichrist is thought long by the saints, being the space of forty two months, or 1260 days or years and this tries the faith and patience of the church of Christ, Rev 13:5. how long shall the wicked triumph? in their prosperity, and in the ruins of the interest of Christ; the Targum is, "how long shall they sit in tranquillity, or prosperity?'' the triumphing of the wicked may seem long, but it is but short, Job 20:5, the inhabitants of the Romish jurisdiction will triumph when the witnesses are slain, and send gifts to one another, as a token of their joy;

94:4 How long shall they utter and speak hard things?,.... Against Christ, his person and offices, his ministers, his people, his truths and ordinances; this is very applicable to antichrist, who has a mouth speaking blasphemies, and which he opens, and with it blasphemes God, his tabernacle, and them that dwell in it, Rev 13:5. The Targum is, "will they utter and speak reproachful words?'' contumelies or calumnies; and such are uttered by the antichristian party against the true professors of religion in great abundance, as water out of a fountain, as the first word (a) used signifies;

94:5 They break in pieces thy people, O Lord,.... Not the Israelites, as Kimchi; but the church of Christ, by their anathemas, cruel edicts, and persecutions; by confiscating their goods, imprisoning their persons, putting them to cruel deaths; and by such means think to "wear out" the saints of the most High, the Lord's covenant and peculiar people; which is mentioned as an aggravation of their sin, and as an argument with the Lord to arise on their behalf: and afflict thine heritage;
viuda... extranjero... huérfanos: la ley del Señor protege a las personas necesitadas ( **Dt 24:17,** **27:19** ), pero siguen siendo vulnerables en la sociedad.
Los malvados creen que pueden salirse con la suya con sus actos y palabras viles, asumiendo ingenuamente que Dios no está observando o no le importa.
El salmista critica la insensatez humana.
Es absurdo que aquellos que tienen oídos y ojos imaginen a su Creador como sordo y ciego.

castiga: esta palabra también conlleva la idea de "advierte" ( **2:10** ), "disciplina" ( **6:1,** **94:12** ), o "instruye" ( **16:7** ). El Señor disciplina a su pueblo para purificar su carácter ( **Pr 3:11-12,** **Hb 12:6-11** ). Él castiga a sus enemigos dándoles lo que merecen ( **Sal 1:6,** **Ro 6:23** ).
Aunque los pensamientos del Señor son profundos, los pensamientos de las personas no tienen valor (cp. **92:5,** **1 Co 3:20** ).
Fiel a su naturaleza, el Señor recompensa a quienes viven de acuerdo con sus sabias instrucciones y los hace felices conforme a sus promesas.
El alivio de Dios abarca su consuelo y su rescate.
Incluso cuando parece que lo ha hecho, el Señor no abandonará a su pueblo.
Fiel a su naturaleza, el Señor recompensa a quienes viven de acuerdo con sus sabias instrucciones y los hace felices conforme a sus promesas.
Solo Dios tiene el poder para enfrentarse a los malvados (ver **2:2** ).
La oración del salmista se reanuda (ver **94:1-7** ) con un grito desesperado por la justicia del Señor y concluye con la esperanza de que el mal llegará a su fin.\n\n
La experiencia del salmista de resbalar contrasta con la promesa de Dios de estar con él ( **94:12-13,** ver **15:5,** **93:1** ).
La experiencia del salmista de resbalar contrasta con la promesa de Dios de estar con él ( **94:12-13,** ver **15:5,** **93:1** ).

94:20 Shall the throne of iniquity have fellowship with thee,.... Or "be joined with thee", be "partner with thee" (f), as antichrist affects to be; who may well be called "the throne of iniquity", since the dragon, the old serpent, and Satan, gave him his power, seat, or throne, and great authority: his coming is after the working of Satan, with all deceivableness of unrighteousness; he sits and enacts, practices and countenances, all manner of iniquity; he sits in the temple of God, showing himself as if he was God;

94:21 They gather themselves together,.... As "in troops" (h), as the word signifies; in great armies; so the antichristian kings and states will, at the instigation of Popish emissaries; see Rev 16:17, against the soul of the righteous; or "the life" of them; in order to take away their lives; to cut them off, root and branch, and destroy at once the whole interest of Christ; for it will be to make war with him, and them his followers, who are the righteous here meant, made so by his righteousness, that they will be gathered together in such great numbers: and condemn the innocent blood;

94:22 But the Lord is my defence,.... The defence of his church and people, of all the righteous, against those great armies of their enemies that gather together against them: the Targum, in the king's Bible is, "the Word of the Lord shall be my weapon:'' and my God is the rock of my refuge; to whom recourse is had for shelter from the enemy, and against which the gates of hell cannot prevail: both characters, rock and refuge, agree with Christ, the essential Word of the Lord.

94:23 And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity,.... The just punishment of it; or cause the mischief they designed to others to fall upon themselves; or make retaliation to them; that whereas they had drank the blood of the saints and martyrs of Jesus, blood should be given them to drink; or their own blood should be shed, Rev 16:6, the Jews (i) say, that the Levites stood on their desks, and recited this passage, both at the precise time of the destruction of the first temple by Nebuchadnezzar, and of the second by the Romans: and shall cut them off in their own wickedness;
